Licensed to the .NET Foundation under one or more agreements.\n// The .NET Foundation licenses this file to you under the MIT license.\n\nusing System.Collections.Concurrent;\nusing System.ComponentModel;\nusing System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;\nusing System.Linq.Expressions;\nusing System.Reflection;\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.HotReload;\n\nnamespace Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Forms;\n\ninternal static class ExpressionMemberAccessor\n{\n    private static readonly ConcurrentDictionary<Expression, MemberInfo> _memberInfoCache = new();\n    private static readonly ConcurrentDictionary<MemberInfo, string> _displayNameCache = new();\n\n    static ExpressionMemberAccessor()\n    {\n        if (HotReloadManager.Default.MetadataUpdateSupported)\n        {\n            HotReloadManager.Default.OnDeltaApplied += ClearCache;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemberInfo GetMemberInfo<TValue>(Expression<Func<TValue>> accessor)\n    {\n        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(accessor);\n\n        return _memberInfoCache.GetOrAdd(accessor, static expr =>\n        {\n            var lambdaExpression = (LambdaExpression)expr;\n            var accessorBody = lambdaExpression.Body;\n\n            if (accessorBody is UnaryExpression unaryExpression\n                && unaryExpression.NodeType == ExpressionType.Convert\n                && unaryExpression.Type == typeof(object))\n            {\n                accessorBody = unaryExpression.Operand;\n            }\n\n            if (accessorBody is not MemberExpression memberExpression)\n            {\n                throw new ArgumentException(\n                    $\"The provided expression contains a {accessorBody.GetType().Name} which is not supported. \" +\n                    $\"Only simple member accessors (fields, properties) of an object are supported.\");\n            }\n\n            return memberExpression.Member;\n        });\n    }\n\n    public static string GetDisplayName(MemberInfo member)\n    {\n        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(member);\n\n        return _displayNameCache.GetOrAdd(member, static m =>\n        {\n            var displayNameAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayNameAttribute>();\n            if (displayNameAttribute?.DisplayName is not null)\n            {\n                return displayNameAttribute.DisplayName;\n            }\n\n            var displayAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayAttribute>();\n            if (displayAttribute is not null)\n            {\n                var name = displayAttribute.GetName();\n                if (name is not null)\n                {\n                    return name;\n                }\n            }\n\n            return m.Name;\n        });\n    }\n\n    public static string GetDisplayName<TValue>(Expression<Func<TValue>> accessor)\n    {\n        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(accessor);\n        var member = GetMemberInfo(accessor);\n        return GetDisplayName(member);\n    }\n\n    private static void ClearCache()\n    {\n        _memberInfoCache.Clear();\n    }\n}\n","numLines":91,"startLine":1,"totalLines":91}}}

For { get; set; }\n    28→    /// <inheritdoc />\n    29→    void IComponent.Attach(RenderHandle renderHandle)\n    30→    {\n    31→        _renderHandle = renderHandle;\n    32→    }\n    33→\n    34→    /// <inheritdoc />\n    35→    Task IComponent.SetParametersAsync(ParameterView parameters)\n    36→    {\n    37→        parameters.SetParameterProperties(this);\n    38→\n    39→        var newDisplayName = ExpressionMemberAccessor.GetDisplayName(For);\n    40→        _displayName = newDisplayName;\n    41→        _renderHandle.Render(BuildRenderTree);\n    42→\n    43→        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    44→    }\n    45→\n    46→    private void BuildRenderTree(RenderTreeBuilder builder)\n    47→    {\n    48→        builder.AddContent(0, _displayName);\n    49→    }\n    50→}\n    51→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6778a53c-927a-427e-8d40-472495acd382","uuid":"9077214d-5cdd-4274-addc-f1b7c7a0f067","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/workspace/src/Components/Web/src/Forms/DisplayName.cs","content":"// Licensed to the .NET Foundation under one or more agreements.\n// The .NET Foundation licenses this file to you under the MIT license.\n\nusing System.ComponentModel;\nusing System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations;\nusing System.Linq.Expressions;\nusing Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Rendering;\n\nnamespace Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Forms;\n\n/// <summary>\n/// Displays the display name for a specified field, reading from <see cref=\"DisplayAttribute\"/>\n/// or <see cref=\"DisplayNameAttribute\"/> if present, or falling back to the property name.\n/// </summary>\n/// <typeparam name=\"TValue\">The type of the field.</typeparam>\npublic class DisplayName<TValue> : IComponent\n{\n\n    private RenderHandle _renderHandle;\n    private Expression<Func<TValue>>? _previousFieldAccessor;\n    private string? _displayName;\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Specifies the field for which the display name should be shown.\n    /// </summary>\n    [Parameter, EditorRequired]\n    public Expression<Func<TValue>>? For { get; set; }\n    /// <inheritdoc />\n    void IComponent.Attach(RenderHandle renderHandle)\n    {\n        _renderHandle = renderHandle;\n    }\n\n    /// <inheritdoc />\n    Task IComponent.SetParametersAsync(ParameterView parameters)\n    {\n        parameters.SetParameterProperties(this);\n\n        var newDisplayName = ExpressionMemberAccessor.GetDisplayName(For);\n        _displayName = newDisplayName;\n        _renderHandle.Render(BuildRenderTree);\n\n        return Task.CompletedTask;\n    }\n\n    private void BuildRenderTree(RenderTreeBuilder builder)\n    {\n        builder.AddContent(0, _displayName);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":51,"startLine":1,"totalLines":51}}}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01LG7W3vAAeACJWakufkkjXG","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Let me analyze both files for defects:\n\n**ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs:**\n\n1. **Wrong attribute precedence** (line 59-73): The code checks `DisplayNameAttribute` first, then `DisplayAttribute`. But ASP.NET Core conventions (and data annotations) typically prefer `[Display]` over `[DisplayName]`, as `[Display]` is the newer, more feature-rich attribute from `System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations`. The correct order should be `[Display]` first, then fall back to `[DisplayName]`. Let me verify this based on how other Blazor form components work...\n   \n   Actually, looking at how `ValidationMessage` and `InputBase` work in ASP.NET Core, they check `DisplayAttribute` first, then `DisplayNameAttribute`. So the precedence in this code is backwards and needs to be flipped.\n\n2. **Missing `_displayNameCache` clear in ClearCache** (line 86-89): The `ClearCache()` method only clears `_memberInfoCache` but doesn't clear `_displayNameCache`. This means cached display names won't be invalidated during hot reload, causing stale values to persist.\n\n3. **Unnecessary re-rendering in SetParametersAsync** (lines 36-43): The method always re-renders even when the `For` expression hasn't changed. There's an unused `_previousFieldAccessor` field that suggests this optimization was planned but never completed—I should compare the current expression against the previous one and only update when it actually changes.\n\n4. **Nullable `For` parameter passed to non-null method**: The `For` property is declared as nullable but gets passed directly to `ExpressionMemberAccessor.GetDisplayName()` which expects a non-null value, creating a potential null reference issue. I've identified three key defects in the code. The first is an attribute precedence issue where `DisplayNameAttribute` is being checked before `DisplayAttribute`, but it should be the other way around. Second, the `ClearCache()` method only clears the member info cache and misses the display name cache, which breaks hot reload functionality. Third, there's an unused field in DisplayName.cs that suggests an optimization for preventing unnecessary re-renders was intentionally left out, causing the component to re-render even when the `For` parameter hasn't changed. Now I'm writing up the review with the specific patches.\n\nReordering the attribute checks...\n\nNow I need to fix the ClearCache method to also clear the display name cache, not just the member info cache. I'm adding a reference equality check to avoid unnecessary re-renders when the field accessor hasn't actually changed, then only updating the display name and triggering a render if there's a real difference. This optimization prevents redundant work in the SetParametersAsync method. Looking at the cache clearing method and the component parameter handling, I'm setting up a way to invalidate the display name cache when needed, and then in the DisplayName component I'm extracting the display name through the accessor and triggering a re-render whenever parameters change.\n\nNow I'm preparing the JSON review file...","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1146,"cache_creation_input_tokens":864,"cache_read_input_tokens":10801,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":864,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":11,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6778a53c-927a-427e-8d40-472495acd382","uuid":"8ba1c76a-b25b-41c6-9429-b2c26ddcb8ed"}

The current order means that a [DisplayName] annotation silently wins over a [Display] annotation, which is the opposite of expected behavior.\",\n    \"fix_patch\": \"--- a/src/Components/Web/src/Forms/ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs\\n+++ b/src/Components/Web/src/Forms/ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs\\n@@ -57,18 +57,18 @@\\n         return _displayNameCache.GetOrAdd(member, static m =>\\n         {\\n-            var displayNameAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayNameAttribute>();\\n-            if (displayNameAttribute?.DisplayName is not null)\\n+            var displayAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayAttribute>();\\n+            if (displayAttribute is not null)\\n             {\\n-                return displayNameAttribute.DisplayName;\\n+                var name = displayAttribute.GetName();\\n+                if (name is not null)\\n+                {\\n+                    return name;\\n+                }\\n             }\\n \\n-            var displayAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayAttribute>();\\n-            if (displayAttribute is not null)\\n+            var displayNameAttribute = m.GetCustomAttribute<DisplayNameAttribute>();\\n+            if (displayNameAttribute?.DisplayName is not null)\\n             {\\n-                var name = displayAttribute.GetName();\\n-                if (name is not null)\\n-                {\\n-                    return name;\\n-                }\\n+                return displayNameAttribute.DisplayName;\\n             }\\n \\n             return m.Name;\\n\"\n  },\n  {\n    \"file\": \"src/Components/Web/src/Forms/ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs\",\n    \"line\": 88,\n    \"severity\": \"critical\",\n    \"description\": \"Incomplete hot reload cache invalidation: ClearCache() only clears _memberInfoCache but not _displayNameCache. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_0194Tvw3599wHfnVuUUCE7ez","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The review is written to `/workspace/review.json`. Here is a summary of the three defects found:\n\n---\n\n## Defect 1 — Wrong attribute precedence (`ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs:59`, severity: `high`)\n\n`[DisplayNameAttribute]` is checked **before** `[DisplayAttribute]`. ASP.NET Core conventions treat `[Display]` (from `System.ComponentModel.DataAnnotations`) as the higher-priority, modern attribute and `[DisplayName]` (from `System.ComponentModel`) as the legacy fallback. The current order silently lets `[DisplayName]` override `[Display]`, which is backward.\n\n**Fix:** Swap the check order — check `DisplayAttribute.GetName()` first, then fall back to `DisplayNameAttribute.DisplayName`, then the member name.\n\n---\n\n## Defect 2 — Incomplete hot reload cache invalidation (`ExpressionMemberAccessor.cs:88`, severity: `critical`)\n\n`ClearCache()` (called on `HotReloadManager.OnDeltaApplied`) only clears `_memberInfoCache` but **not** `_displayNameCache`. After a hot reload that changes an attribute like `[Display(Name=\"...\")]`, the stale display name string remains cached indefinitely, making the feature invisible to hot reload.\n\n**Fix:** Add `_displayNameCache.Clear();` to `ClearCache()`.\n\n---\n\n## Defect 3 — Missing render optimization (`DisplayName.cs:39`, severity: `high`)\n\n`SetParametersAsync` unconditionally recomputes the display name and calls `_renderHandle.Render` on **every** parameter update. The `_previousFieldAccessor` field is declared on line 20 precisely for a change-check guard but is never used. Blazor `IComponent` implementations are expected to skip re-rendering when inputs haven't changed.\n\n**Fix:** Wrap the recompute+render in `if (!ReferenceEquals(For, _previousFieldAccessor))` and update `_previousFieldAccessor` inside the branch."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2271,"cache_read_input_tokens":15472,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2271,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"6778a53c-927a-427e-8d40-472495acd382","uuid":"dd2cf005-afec-4491-8e57-f3c73fcf1781"}
